title: Developing an American identity
description: Practice your skills in this quiz on American culture in the 1790s

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Could these colonists who had been British and who had celebrated their Britishness for generations become a truly independent people? How could one united people descended from the same ancestors, speaking the same language, and professing the same Protestant religion differentiate themselves from the people of the former mother country? These questions, more than any others, bedeviled the politics of the early decades of the new Republic’s history.”

>-Source: Gordon S. Wood, *Empire of Liberty: A History of the Early Republic, 1789-1815,* 2009

**Which of the following cultural shifts resulted most directly from the trends described in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: development of a distinct national culture in the United States
adoption of an anglicized culture by Northern and Southern Americans
rise of female artists and architects to build and maintain the national culture
formation of a two-party political system

assessment_items.question_content:
> “Could these colonists who had been British and who had celebrated their Britishness for generations become a truly independent people? How could one united people descended from the same ancestors, speaking the same language, and professing the same Protestant religion differentiate themselves from the people of the former mother country? These questions, more than any others, bedeviled the politics of the early decades of the new Republic’s history.”

>-Source: Gordon S. Wood, *Empire of Liberty: A History of the Early Republic, 1789-1815,* 2009

**Which of the following statements is most consistent with the author’s argument in this passage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Americans sought to create a culture that was distinct from British culture
Americans sought to adopt aspects of British and French culture
Americans sought to create a culture that was based off Ancient Greece
Americans sought to adopt aspects of local Indigenous cultures

assessment_items.question_content:
> “It is certainly much to be regretted that party discriminations are so far Geographical as they have been; and that ideas of a severance of the Union are creeping in both North and South. In the South it is supposed that more government than is expedient is desired by the North. In the North, it is believed, that the prejudices of the South are incompatible with the necessary degree of Government and with the attainment of the essential ends of National Union. In both quarters there are respectable men who talk of separation, as a thing dictated by the different geniusses [sic] and different prejudices of the parts. But happily their number is not considerable—& the prevailing sentiment of the people is in favour of their true interest, union.”

>-Source: Alexander Hamilton, in a letter to George Washington, [Founders Online National Archives](https://founders.archives.gov/documents/Hamilton/01-12-02-0184-0002), 1792

**Based on the text, which of the following statements would the author most likely agree with?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: While a new national identity emerged in the United States, variations appeared in the North and South.
Northern and Southern regional identities began to resemble each other after the American Revolution.
Geographical distinctions had no influence on the development of a new national culture.
Foreign powers sought to capitalize on regional conflict to make it easier to conquer the United States.

assessment_items.question_content:
> “It is certainly much to be regretted that party discriminations are so far Geographical as they have been; and that ideas of a severance of the Union are creeping in both North and South. In the South it is supposed that more government than is expedient is desired by the North. In the North, it is believed, that the prejudices of the South are incompatible with the necessary degree of Government and with the attainment of the essential ends of National Union. In both quarters there are respectable men who talk of separation, as a thing dictated by the different geniusses [sic] and different prejudices of the parts. But happily their number is not considerable—& the prevailing sentiment of the people is in favour of their true interest, union.”

>-Source: Alexander Hamilton, in a letter to George Washington, [Founders Online National Archives](https://founders.archives.gov/documents/Hamilton/01-12-02-0184-0002), 1792

**Which of the following events best represents a continuity of the sentiments expressed in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the drafting of the Kentucky and Virginia Resolutions
the Enlightenment
the XYZ Affair
the Revolution of 1800

assessment_items.question_content:
> “—The most powerful motive I had or have for engaging in, or continuing the study of painting has been, the wish of commemorating the great Events of our Countrys [sic] Revolution: I am fully sensible that the profession as it is generally practised, is frivolous, little useful to Society, and unworthy the attention of a Man who possesses talents for more serious occupations—but, to diffuse the knowledge and preserve the Memory of the noblest series of Actions which have ever dignified the History of Man:—to give to the present and the future Sons of Oppression and Misfortune such glorious Lessons of their rights and of the Spirit with which they should assert and support them:—and even to transmit to their descendants the personal resemblance of those who have been the great actors in these illustrious scenes. . . .”

>-Source: John Trumbell, In a letter to Thomas Jefferson, https://founders.archives.gov/documents/Jefferson/01-15-02-0183, 1789

**The ideas expressed in the excerpt are more closely aligned with which of the following broader historical developments of the 1790s?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The creation of new works of art, literature, and architecture that expressed ideas about American national identity.
The renewed commitment to the education of women so they can teach their sons how to be good citizens.
The increased participation in the transcendentalist movement by authors and painters.
The growth in funding for the arts to help the United States recover after a financial crisis.

assessment_items.question_content:
> “—The most powerful motive I had or have for engaging in, or continuing the study of painting has been, the wish of commemorating the great Events of our Countrys [sic] Revolution: I am fully sensible that the profession as it is generally practised, is frivolous, little useful to Society, and unworthy the attention of a Man who possesses talents for more serious occupations—but, to diffuse the knowledge and preserve the Memory of the noblest series of Actions which have ever dignified the History of Man:—to give to the present and the future Sons of Oppression and Misfortune such glorious Lessons of their rights and of the Spirit with which they should assert and support them:—and even to transmit to their descendants the personal resemblance of those who have been the great actors in these illustrious scenes. . . .”

>-Source: John Trumbell, In a letter to Thomas Jefferson, https://founders.archives.gov/documents/Jefferson/01-15-02-0183, 1789

**Arguments similar to those expressed in the excerpt were later employed to justify which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: creation of government programs that funded the arts
extension of suffrage to all white men
formation of a Progressive movement
emergence of the two-party system

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d221072da24a8a2d0c8c069299bec404.jpg)

>-Source: John Trumbull, *The Declaration of Independence, July 4, 1776,*[Wikimedia Commons,](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/f9/Declaration_of_Independence_%281819%29%2C_by_John_Trumbull.jpg) 1819

**The painting is best seen as evidence for which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: emergence of an American national identity
growing political participation of impoverished white men
rise in public education for white women
support for the American Revolution

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/d221072da24a8a2d0c8c069299bec404.jpg)

>-Source: John Trumbull, *The Declaration of Independence, July 4, 1776,*[Wikimedia Commons,](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/f/f9/Declaration_of_Independence_%281819%29%2C_by_John_Trumbull.jpg) 1819

**What was the intention of paintings similar to the one above?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: to encourage patriotism and the development of American national identity
to memorialize those lost in the American Revolution
to convince loyalists to support the American Revolution
to convince countries like France and Spain to engage in peacetime alliances with the United States

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5a661efc170ca44dd6b90237865a3515.jpg)

>-Source: John Plumbe, United States capitol, [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:United_States_Capitol,_circa_1846,_by_John_Plumbe.jpg), 1846

**The creation of the US Capitol as depicted in the image demonstrates which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: use of architecture to portray a new national identity
incorporation of British culture into American architecture
financial difficulties that the United States suffered after the American Revolution
regional variations on American culture

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/5a661efc170ca44dd6b90237865a3515.jpg)

>-Source: John Plumbe, United States capitol, [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:United_States_Capitol,_circa_1846,_by_John_Plumbe.jpg), 1846

**The architecture in the image is best seen as evidence for which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The emergence of a new national culture that was distinct from British culture.
The success of Americans of the United States to differentiate their culture from surrounding Indigenous nations.
The lasting impact of British culture on the United States following the American Revolution.
The physical devastation left after battles during the American Revolution.

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8931a0b25350e4335ac9b5ee66067118.jpg)

>-Source: Edward Savage, “Liberty in the Form of the goddess of Youth Giving Support to the Bald Eagle,” [Library of Congress](https://www.loc.gov/pictures/item/2003689261/), 1796

**The painting can best be used as evidence for which of the following US historical trends that took place after the American Revolution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: use of art to depict ideas about national identity that was distinct from British culture
support for American participation in conflicts between France and Great Britain
endorsement of women to commit to the study of art
promotion of the right to vote for women

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/8931a0b25350e4335ac9b5ee66067118.jpg)

>-Source: Edward Savage, “Liberty in the Form of the goddess of Youth Giving Support to the Bald Eagle,” [Library of Congress](https://www.loc.gov/pictures/item/2003689261/), 1796

**The image most strongly supports which of the following arguments about the United States after the American Revolution?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: how artists, architects, and authors used their art forms to depict and create a new American culture
how the culture of the United States was anglicized in the years after the American Revolution
how Indigenous communities’ cultural values influenced the new culture of the United States
how art movements became focused on depicting the failings of the United States government
